topics.title: The Sun
topics.description: The Sun is a star at the center of our solar system. Its gravity holds the planets in orbit, and its light gives Earth the energy needed for weather, plants, and life.
topics.imageAlt: A bright illustrated Sun with warm rays.
topics.caption: The Sun contains most of the solar system's mass.

quiz.question: What keeps the planets moving around the Sun?

topics.title: Earth
topics.description: Earth is the rocky planet we call home. Liquid water, a protective atmosphere, and a steady supply of sunlight make it the only known world with life.
topics.imageAlt: An illustrated blue and green Earth.
topics.caption: Earth's atmosphere helps keep temperatures livable.

quiz.question: About how long does Earth take to orbit the Sun?

topics.title: Mars
topics.description: Mars is a cold desert world with rusty soil, polar ice caps, and the tallest volcano in the solar system. Robotic explorers study it for clues about ancient water.
topics.caption: Iron-rich dust gives Mars its red color.

topics.title: Jupiter
topics.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. It is a gas giant with powerful storms, dozens of moons, and a famous storm called the Great Red Spot.
topics.caption: Jupiter is so large that more than 1,300 Earths could fit inside it.

quiz.question: What kind of planet is Jupiter?

topics.title: Saturn
topics.description: Saturn is a gas giant known for its wide ring system. The rings are made of countless pieces of ice and rock, from tiny grains to boulder-sized chunks.
topics.imageAlt: An illustrated Saturn with a wide ring.
topics.caption: Saturn's rings are thin compared with their enormous width.

quiz.question: What are Saturn's rings mostly made from?
